results.title: Study Finds Widespread AI Complaints
results.content:
## Key findings:

 Of nearly 20,000 business reviews mentioning AI since 2023, more than 90% indicated negative sentiment.
 BBB has received nearly 100,000 AI and automation-related complaints since 2023 with many in online retail, ecommerce, and tech-forward industries.
 Consumers expressed confusion and frustration with an inability to solve their issues.
 Many consumers felt ignored or left behind when not able to connect with a human representative.

## What can businesses do to integrate AI successfully? [...] |  |  |  |  |

# Study Finds Widespread AI Complaints

|  |  |
 --- |
| By: M. Scott Morris | 512 Reads |  Add Franchising.com |  |

Over the past three years, BBB has received more than 100,000 complaints, reviews, and Scam Tracker reports mentioning artificial intelligence (AI) and automation. While reporting categories encompass different types of issues, there is one clear concern across all types of consumer sentiment data: Customers often feel unheard or dissatisfied when dealing with automated and AI services. [...] In the new study “As Technology Booms, Over 90% of BBB Customer Reviews That Mention AI Services Express Negative Experience,” BBB’s International Investigations Initiative (III) examines patterns of reports and shares stories to empower businesses to research whether AI tools are the right choice for their company and to prepare consumers to distinguish legitimate businesses from scams.

## Key findings:

  Just think how frustrated you get when you contact a business & find yourself chatting with a bot Same is AI generated content, it is Cookie - Cutter content that has a signature style For a Website or a Sales Blog, AI content is not healthy. For talking to people, human is required. Writing for real audience, human writers are the key. Businesses with AI content on Website 👇 - Nothing new to offer - ⁠No human to understand customer expectations - ⁠Already existing words rephrased with synonyms .............. 🛎️Hoping to see more digital content ❗️Not digitally created content Thnx for reading till here 🫶🏻

  38   13 Comments

  Like   Comment [...] Riley Kaminer

 Report this post

Content marketing is broken. AI made it faster. But not better. Most of what’s out there? Predictable. Forgettable. The fix isn’t more content. It’s better storytelling. Not marketing… journalism. Ask questions no one else is asking. Dig deeper than SEO keywords. Talk to real people. Share their stories. Because people don’t trust algorithms. They trust people. Great brands win by being curious, investigative, human. Not by following formulas. Stop thinking like a marketer. Start thinking like a reporter. That’s how content resonates. That’s how trust is built. 👉 Let’s chat if you’re ready to pivot.

## More Relevant Posts

 Lucas Wollschlager

  + Report this post [...] I analyzed 500 AI-generated ads last month. 99% mentioned these words: → "Revolutionary" → "Game-changing" → "Cutting-edge" → "Innovative" You know what percentage of humans actually use these words? About 0.2%. We're creating a generation of marketers who sound like robots talking to humans. Meanwhile, the highest-converting ad I wrote last month started with: "This thing is ugly as hell, but it works." No superlatives. No buzzwords. Just honesty. It outperformed the "revolutionary, game-changing solution" by 340%. The insight? Humans trust humans who sound human. Not humans who sound like they swallowed a marketing textbook. AI is making everyone sound the same. The opportunity? Sound different. What "ugly truth" could you lead with in your copy? #Copywriting #AIMarketing

So there are hundreds of AI marketing tools out there, but I just discovered one that can replace your entire marketing team. So it's like, I've done all this work of like all these hundreds of AI tools. This is the one that I would recommend. Established the video plus a personal connection. As someone who's built multiple apps, I always struggled with marketing. I'd either spend thousands on agencies or waste hours trying to do them do it myself. But what I found with Poppy AI completely changed how I think about marketing. I would change that to like marketing with AI basically. So don't lie in [...] their AI app idea. But here's the brutal truth. The best AI apps are the ones that launched, broken, and iterated fast. So, this would have taken me ages to come up with off the top, like by myself. And what is great here is that it's following the exact hook structure that I want to use and the exact format that I want to actually create these videos in as well. And what we could do from this is actually get it to write out a script based on one of these ideas. So, let's say everyone says the AI app market is too saturated, but get this, 99% of AI apps solve the wrong problem entirely. I'm not 100% loving that one. I think this one is really good. The validation trap. Let's say let's write a script using my tone of voice for the validation trap idea. It's called validation trap. I should [...] tweaking that mockup clock fast forwarding through weeks. Most people spend months perfecting their AI app idea, researching competitors, building the perfect feature set. But here's the truth. The best AI apps are the ones that launched broken and iterated fast. I'm going to show you exactly how to validate your AI app idea in one day, not one month. First, build the absolute minimum version of your idea. I'm talking bare bones one core AI feature, basic interface. Launch it immediately and watch what people do with it. Here's what I've learned. After building 50 plus AI apps, users will tell you exactly what they want, but only after they can touch it. results.title: Why AI marketing tools aren't enough: the missing skills | Chris Toy posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
maintain transparency with stakeholders about where AI is involved (people can spot AI from a mile away these days and many are still weary about the use of it!!) If you’re experimenting with AI in areas of your marketing (content, ad automation, customer insights), I’d love to hear your AI “wins” and your AI “uh-oh’s” 🫣 If you wanna chat about how we can strategies together or just talk about all things AI, book a time with me using my link in bio OR direct message me!! Let’s keep breaking new groud – let's do it together!! [...] up your A/B testing! It frees you from wasting your time doing busywork so your mind can focus on strategy instead of being burnout. \Predictive analytics over historical reporting Rather than just reflecting what DID happen, AI lets us forecast what WILL happen. You can allocate budget more confidently, detect anomalies before they blow up, or pivot campaigns proactively! !!!A WORD OF CAUTION!!! AI is powerful, yes, but is it magic? NO! Is it always 100% accurate? ALSO NO! ALWAYS validate outputs with human judgment (ai isn't 100% accurate at all times!!) ALWAYS watch out for bias or “hallucinations” in the data (chat gpt likes to spit out false information a lot, it only works as good as what the human is inputing) ALWAYS maintain transparency with stakeholders about where AI is [...] Preston M.
